heading: Theft of trade secrets
label: 1832 Theft of trade secrets
notes: Amendments2016—Subsec. (b). substituted “the greater of $5,000,000 or 3 times the value of the stolen trade secret to the organization, including expenses for research and design and other costs of reproducing the trade secret that the organization has thereby avoided” for “$5,000,000”.Pub. L. 114–1532012—Subsec. (a). substituted “a product or service used in or intended for use in” for “or included in a product that is produced for or placed in” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 112–236

Report on Theft of Trade Secrets Occurring Abroad, , , which requires biannual reports on the theft of trade secrets of United States companies occurring outside of the United States, was editorially reclassified as , Crime Control and Law Enforcement.Pub. L. 114–153, § 4130 Stat. 382section 41310 of Title 34May 11, 2016

children.text: steals, or without authorization appropriates, takes, carries away, or conceals, or by fraud, artifice, or deception obtains such information;

without authorization copies, duplicates, sketches, draws, photographs, downloads, uploads, alters, destroys, photocopies, replicates, transmits, delivers, sends, mails, communicates, or conveys such information;
receives, buys, or possesses such information, knowing the same to have been stolen or appropriated, obtained, or converted without authorization;
attempts to commit any offense described in paragraphs (1) through (3); or
conspires with one or more other persons to commit any offense described in paragraphs (1) through (3), and one or more of such persons do any act to effect the object of the conspiracy,

children.label: b
children.text: Any organization that commits any offense described in subsection (a) shall be fined not more than the greater of $5,000,000 or 3 times the value of the stolen trade secret to the organization, including expenses for research and design and other costs of reproducing the trade secret that the organization has thereby avoided.
